name: English
target_style:
Expected style:
- Neutral, measured journalistic tone, neither too casual nor too academic
- Short sentences, oral rhythm but without exclamations or prompts like "Listen up!"
- Avoid excessive use of "you guys"; prefer "we" or impersonal forms
- Always end on a complete sentence, never mid-thought
- Prioritize facts and concrete examples over general opinions

fallback.conclusion: That concludes our podcast about {topic}. Thank you for listening.

prompts.system_data:
Here is some context for the prompts: 
{context}
prompts.topic_choice:
Choose the most original, precise and realistic title for an English podcast.
Avoid titles that are too generic or too close to 'The evolution of X in 2026'.
Titles:
{topics}

Rules:
- Reply with ONLY the chosen title.
- If none are suitable, reply with exactly: [REGENERATE]
prompts.outline_generation:
Create a plan for a podcast episode in English.
Precise topic: {topic}

ABSOLUTE RULES:
- 4 to 6 DISTINCT sections, with no thematic overlap
- Each section must adopt a DIFFERENT angle: a cause, a consequence, an actor, a tension, a concrete case, a time period, a comparison...
- FORBIDDEN to have two sections on the same angle (e.g. two economic sections, two technical sections)
- Each title must contain a verb or express a tension
- 4 to 8 words per title
- No markdown, no explanations, only the titles

MANDATORY FORMAT:
[P]Title[/P]
[P]Title[/P]
prompts.intro_generation:
You are a radio journalist.
Write the introduction for a podcast about: {topic}
The podcast will cover: {outline_str}
{style}

Rules:
- 50 to 100 words
- Strong hook from the very first sentence
- Don't give a name to the podcast
- Clear outline of the plan without detailing it
- No staging (music, etc.)
- End with a complete sentence before [/I]

Format:
[I]
Text
[/I]
prompts.conclusion_generation:
You are a radio journalist.
Write the conclusion for a podcast about: {topic}
Here is a summary of points already covered (DO NOT repeat or rephrase):
{key_points}
{style}

Rules:
- 50 to 100 words
- Do NOT summarize what was just said: open onto a perspective, an open question, or a future challenge
- No repetition of examples or names already cited
- End with a complete sentence before [/C]

Format:
[C]
Text
[/C]
prompts.resume_section_keywords:
Read this text and extract ONLY the most concrete and distinctive examples:
- Proper nouns cited (people, institutions, products, organizations)
- Titles of named works, technologies, or precise standards
- Unique concrete examples (e.g. 'kimchi', 'NIH', 'Lactobacillus reuteri')
ABSOLUTE RULE: maximum 10 elements. No abstract concepts. No generic terms.
Format: one item per line, no sentence:

{text}
prompts.section_verify:
You are a strict reviewer.
Here is a list of SPECIFIC forbidden elements (proper nouns, precise examples, distinctive concepts):
{forbidden}

Here is a text to verify:
{text}

Does the text explicitly mention any of these forbidden elements?
IMPORTANT: ignore coincidental matches on generic or domain-unavoidable words.
Only flag a violation if a forbidden element is clearly reused.
Reply ONLY with:
[OK] if no clear violation
[FAIL]term1, term2[/FAIL] if specific forbidden elements are present

log_messages.no_tty: No interactive terminal detected, defaulting to English.
log_messages.unsupported_lang: Unsupported language '{lang}', falling back to English.

angles: practical or concrete (use case, real example)
